policies.name: Industrial and Investment Policy of Assam, 2019 (IIPA 2019)
policies.what_companies_get: SGST reimbursement; power subsidy; generating-set subsidy; stamp duty reimbursement; technology-transfer & quality-certification assistance; interest subsidy on working-capital loans; financial assistance for MSMEs listing on stock exchanges; environmental-compliance assistance; incentives to private-sector infrastructure developers. Percentages/caps are in the operational guidelines PDF (hosted on both official sites), not restated on the incentive page.
policies.status: In force; no 2025 successor policy found on either official site (EODB site last updated 01-Feb-2025; Advantage Assam 2.0 summit Feb 2025 referenced but no new umbrella policy posted)
policies.application_status: Claims via EODB single-window investor login; 'Know Your Incentives' wizard (eodbmis.assam.gov.in/kyi_incentives/kyi) and a public 'Incentives Granted Dashboard' are live
policies.site_notes: Policy PDF at eodb.assam.gov.in/storage/documents/industrial_and_investment_policy_of_assam_2019.pdf; operational guidelines also on industriescom.assam.gov.in/documents-detail/operational-guidelines-for-industrial-and-investment-policy-of-assam-2019

policies.name: Assam Industrial & Investment Policy (Amendment) 2023 — Mega Investment customized package
policies.what_companies_get: For investments of min Rs 100 crore: capital investment/production-linked subsidy, power-tariff subsidy, land subsidy, subsidy on drawl of power lines, stamp duty reimbursement, employment/payroll subsidy, road-connectivity assistance, utility support — negotiated case-by-case
policies.status: In force (amendment to IIPA 2019)
policies.application_status: Via EODB single window; case-by-case approval
policies.site_notes: PDF: eodb.assam.gov.in/storage/documents/24-25/1735540879218-2-Assam-Industrial-and-Investment-Policy-(Amendment)-2023.pdf; AIIDC page also linked

policies.name: Assam Electronics (Semiconductor etc.) Policy 2023
policies.incentive_type: State top-up package for ISM-approved semiconductor/electronics projects (the state package around Tata's OSAT at Jagiroad/Morigaon)
policies.what_companies_get: Capital subsidy = ADDITIONAL 40% of the GoI (India Semiconductor Mission) capex assistance, disbursed 20%/yr over 5 years (adjustable 18-22%/yr); land at nominal price with freehold transfer case-by-case; 100% stamp duty exemption on govt/AIDC/AIIDC-allotted land; power-tariff incentive up to 50% for 10 years from COD; water at Rs 5/cubic meter for first 10 years (further up to 100% water-tariff subsidy case-by-case); SGST reimbursement up to max 15 years case-by-case; payroll assistance 20% of net wages for local employees; further customized incentives (green energy, accommodation, connectivity) via Empowered Committee with Cabinet approval. Eligible: ISM-qualified fabs (any node), display fabs (TFT-LCD Gen-8+, AMOLED Gen-6+), compound semi/silicon photonics/sensor fabs, ATMP/OSAT. Commissioning window 3y MSME / 5y large
policies.status: Notified 29-08-2023 (ECF No. 358679/1), in force 5 years from notification
policies.application_status: Via Empowered Committee chaired by Minister IC&PE; no separate portal — routed through single window
policies.site_notes: Full 15-page gazette PDF read; EODB semiconductor page (eodb.assam.gov.in/site/semiconductor_industries) confirms Tata TSAT Jagiroad context (Rs 27,000 cr, 48M chips/day) but the page itself describes no incentives

policies.name: Assam Ethanol Production Promotion Policy 2021
policies.what_companies_get: Full incentive schedule in policy PDF hosted on EODB portal (not restated inline on the page); listed as an active sector policy
policies.status: In force, listed among current policies
policies.application_status: Via EODB single window
policies.site_notes: PDF verified present; contents not extracted in this pass

policies.name: Assam Bamboo and Cane Policy 2019 & 2021 (+ National Bamboo Mission assistance)
policies.what_companies_get: Bamboo & Cane Policy PDFs (2019 and 2021 versions) on EODB; separate 'Financial assistance under National Bamboo Mission' application track on industriescom.assam.gov.in
policies.status: In force (2021 version listed as validated 15-Jul-2021)
policies.application_status: NBM assistance applications via industriescom.assam.gov.in information-services section
policies.site_notes: Also industriescom.assam.gov.in/information-services/financial-assistance-under-national-bamboo-mission

policies.name: Assam Startup Policy 2018 (and 2017 predecessor)
policies.what_companies_get: Policy PDF on EODB; dedicated 'Assam Startup' information hub linked from portal
policies.status: Listed as current (2018) on policy page
policies.application_status: Via Assam Startup hub (eodb.assam.gov.in/site/startup_assam_information_hub)
policies.site_notes: Both 2017 and 2018 PDFs hosted

policies.name: UNNATI 2024 (central, cross-referenced by Assam)
policies.what_companies_get: Capital Investment Incentive (CII), Capital Interest Subvention (CIS), Manufacturing & Services Linked Incentive (MSLI) — Assam's incentive page presents it alongside state incentives and links the DPIIT gazette
policies.status: Active; NEIDS 2017 guidelines also still hosted on industriescom.assam.gov.in
policies.application_status: Central portal (unnati.dpiit.gov.in)
policies.site_notes: Confirms the state cross-references UNNATI as complement to IIPA 2019/Amendment 2023; no explicit Assam state top-up to UNNATI stated

policies.name: Meghalaya Industrial & Investment Promotion Policy, 2024 (MIIPP 2024)
policies.what_companies_get: Current policy PDF (1.44 MB) hosted; incentive schedule inside PDF, not restated on page (brief pass — not extracted)
policies.status: Current policy, supersedes MIIPP 2012/MIIPS 2016
policies.application_status: Single Window Agency listed under Citizen Corners
policies.site_notes: Older instruments also hosted: MIIPS 2016, MIIPP 2012, Startup Policy 2018, MSE Procurement Preference Policy 2020

policies.name: UNNATI 2024 implementation (state steering committee)
policies.what_companies_get: State-level Steering Committee actively processing units — homepage carries 'Minutes of the 8th Meeting of the Steering Committee under UNNATI, 2024' plus public notice and list of units
policies.status: Actively implemented
policies.application_status: Registration via central UNNATI portal; state steering committee vetting live
policies.site_notes: UNNATI is the dominant incentive instrument visible; no state top-up scheme found on site

policies.name: Tripura Industrial Investment Promotion Incentive Scheme (TIIPIS), 2017
policies.what_companies_get: Incentive Eligibility Certificate regime; a live notification on the homepage covers submission of offline claims under 'TIIPIS, 17' — scheme is being administered; detailed rates are in scheme PDFs not rendered inline
policies.status: Active (claims notification current)
policies.application_status: Claims for Incentive Eligibility Certificate accepted (offline claim notification posted); EODB 'SWAAGAT' single-window section on site
policies.site_notes: Incentives section splits Central Incentives (links to ncog.gov.in/neind NE incentive portal) vs State Incentives; state-incentives page rendered as navigation shell in fetch — detailed schedule not extractable without JS

policies.name: Land Allotment Policy + Land Allotment Rates
policies.what_companies_get: Allotment in Industrial Growth Centres, Industrial/Bamboo/Export Promotion/Food/Rubber parks and SEZ at published rates
policies.status: Active
policies.application_status: Via department; SWAAGAT single window for approvals
policies.site_notes: Sector pages: rubber, bamboo, food processing, natural gas, tea listed as thrust sectors

policies.name: The Industrial and Investment Policy of Mizoram 2025
policies.what_companies_get: Newly listed 2025 policy (separate 'The Industrial Policy of Mizoram 2025' also listed, one marked draft in URL); detail schedule in policy pages — brief pass, not extracted
policies.status: Notified/listed 2025 alongside legacy Mizoram Industrial Policy 2012
policies.application_status: Via Commerce & Industries Dept / DCICs
policies.site_notes: Also: Mizoram Procurement Preference Policy for MSEs 2025, State Logistics Policy 2022, Mizoram EoDB Rules 2023

policies.name: State incentive subsidies under Mizoram Industrial Policy 2012 (still published schedule)
policies.what_companies_get: Project report cost subsidy: 90% micro (cap Rs 5,000), 75% small (cap Rs 25,000), 50% medium (cap Rs 50,000); land subsidy for units in notified industrial areas; further heads listed on page. Application forms Rs 50 each from directorate
policies.status: Published as current 'State Government Incentives' page (2012-policy schedule; likely to be superseded by 2025 policy)
policies.application_status: Prescribed paper forms via directorate
policies.site_notes: State handholding scheme 'Bana Kaih' and SEDP also active; UNNATI 2024 overview + Model DPR pages hosted (central scheme cross-referenced, no state top-up stated)

policies.name: UNNATI 2024 (state implementation)
policies.incentive_type: Central capital/interest/production incentives — the dominant instrument
policies.what_companies_get: Subsidies and interest assistance under UNNATI; min investment Rs 1 crore (manufacturing) / Rs 50 lakh (services); scheme duration 10 years + 8 years for committed liabilities; Rs 10,037 crore total NE outlay; NEDFi and SIDBI nodal disbursal agencies; 18 model DPRs published by the state (fish/dry-fruit/rubber processing, cold storage, medical devices, resorts, film/VFX studios, EV charging, etc.). Registration window 09-Mar-2024 to 31-Mar-2026
policies.status: Active, FY2024-25 to FY2033-34
policies.application_status: Registration open until 31-Mar-2026 via central portal; state provides model DPRs
policies.site_notes: Dedicated UNNATI page verified; site has Policies & Acts section but no new state-specific incentive scheme surfaced in brief pass. Note: dic.nagaland.gov.in does not exist — correct domain is industry.nagaland.gov.in

policies.name: Industrial and Investment Policy of Manipur, 2013 (and Industrial Policy 1996)
policies.what_companies_get: Policy PDFs are indexed on dcimanipur.gov.in (search-verified paths /media/filer_public/...), but the site refused connections during verification — contents not confirmed live
policies.status: Unverified — site down
policies.application_status: Unknown
policies.site_notes: DNS resolves to NIC IP 164.100.234.15 but HTTPS connection refused and HTTP silent; dtci.manipur.gov.in is NXDOMAIN. Manipur is a UNNATI 2024 covered state per other NE state sites

policies.name: None verifiable
policies.what_companies_get: Site is a React/Vite single-page app returning an empty HTML shell to non-JS clients; no policy content extractable. Covered by UNNATI 2024 (per DPIIT gazette and other NE state sites)
policies.status: Unverified
policies.application_status: Unknown
policies.site_notes: Not WordPress, so the /cms/wp-json headless-WP probe is inapplicable; would need a rendering browser

policies.name: Schemes & Guidelines (central schemes only) + UNNATI promotion
policies.what_companies_get: Site lists ODOP, SC-ST Hub, MSE-CDP guidelines and industrial licence application/renewal; news items show active UNNATI 2024 awareness programmes. NO state-specific incentive scheme for the pharma manufacturing hub found on the site beyond legacy NEIDS/central instruments — 'Invest In Sikkim' page exists but no state top-up scheme is published
policies.status: Central schemes active; no state incentive scheme verified
policies.application_status: Industrial licence application/renewal online via site
policies.site_notes: Answer to brief question: no state pharma incentive beyond legacy NEIDS/central schemes is visible on the official site

policies.name: New Central Sector Scheme (NCSS) 2021 (F.No.1(1)/2020-SSS, DPIIT-administered, applied via J&K)
policies.incentive_type: Capital investment + interest subvention + GST-linked incentive
policies.what_companies_get: Capital Investment Incentive: 30% in Zone A (max Rs 5 cr), 50% in Zone B (max Rs 7.5 cr) — for new units and existing units undertaking substantial expansion with P&M investment up to Rs 50 cr (mfg) or building/durable assets >= Rs 1 cr (services). Capital Interest Subvention: 6% p.a. for max 7 consecutive years on term-loan principal up to Rs 500 cr. GST-Linked Incentive: 100% of gross GST paid (cash + ITC) for max 10 years from commercial production, capped at 300% of P&M investment. Working Capital Interest Subvention for existing units: stated as 5% for 5 years max Rs 1 cr on one slide and 5.5% for 5 years max Rs 5 cr on the scheme table — the official PDF is internally inconsistent on this head
policies.status: Listed as notified 2021 and presented as the flagship incentive on the live portal; scheme validity to 2037 per DPIIT gazette (registration-window status not restated on this portal — not verified here)
policies.application_status: Portal's 'Know Your Incentive' wizard and Incentive Calculator are live and map applicants to applicable incentives
policies.site_notes: The Rs 28,400 cr outlay from the brief is not stated in the portal PDF pages read; zone A/B lists linked in-document

policies.name: Jammu and Kashmir Industrial Policy 2021-30 (GO 117-IND, effective 01.04.2021, 10 years)
policies.incentive_type: State fiscal incentive package (stacked on NCSS)
policies.what_companies_get: 100% stamp duty and court-fee exemption for new units; 100% subsidy on new DG sets 10KW-2000KW (cap Rs 40 lakh Zone A / Rs 45 lakh Zone B); 30% on quality certification (max Rs 2 lakh); 25% on automation (max Rs 2 lakh, if not claimed under CII); 60% on pollution-control devices (max Rs 50 lakh); 50% on green & environment protection initiatives; turnover incentive for existing units for 5 years — 3% micro (max Rs 10 lakh), 2% small/medium/large (max Rs 50 lakh); 100% net SGST reimbursement for 10 years from 01.04.2021. Applies to all industries incl. commercial and institutional units
policies.status: In force to 2031
policies.application_status: Incentive applications through the single-window Know Your Incentive flow tied to 'Incentives under Jammu and Kashmir Industrial Policy, 2021-30'
policies.site_notes: KYI wizard collects unit type, P&M slab, turnover slab, district/block/zone (A/B), sector, negative-list check, then lists applicable subsidies with SOP and Apply links

policies.name: J&K Industrial Land Allotment Policy 2021 (GO 65-IND, 24.03.2021, 10 years)
policies.what_companies_get: Land in industrial estates on 40-year lease extendible to 99 years; DPR appraisal within 30 days; application fees Rs 10,000 (<1 acre), Rs 25,000 (1-5 acres), Rs 50,000 (>5 acres); allotment committees by project size — Divisional (<Rs 50 cr), High Level (Rs 50-200 cr), Apex (>Rs 200 cr); online allotment cycle 75 days; 100% land premium payable within 60 days via portal
policies.status: Actively administered — portal carries High Level Land Allotment Committee minutes (May 2026), relocation lists (Jun 2026), provisional merit lists for Jammu and Kashmir divisions (Apr 2026)
policies.application_status: Live: land bank, GIS land bank, MIS land application, private-land requisition dashboards all online
policies.site_notes: Brief's 'private industrial estate policy' as a named document was not found; portal does have Private Land Bank / Land Requisition dashboards (singlewindow.jk.gov.in/privateLandDashboard/privateLandBank)

policies.name: J&K IT/ITeS Policy 2020 (GO 41-JK(ITD), effective 21.08.2020, 10 years)
policies.what_companies_get: 25% lease-rental reimbursement in govt IT parks (max Rs 10 lakh/yr for 5 yrs); FAR relaxation up to 50% in IT parks / 25% elsewhere; 50% technology-acquisition reimbursement (max Rs 1 cr once in 5 yrs); 25% bandwidth subsidy (max Rs 2 lakh/yr); recruitment assistance Rs 26,000 per employee/yr (min 50 new hires, max 500/yr, for 5 yrs); patent-filing and 30% quality-certificate reimbursement (max Rs 2 lakh); industry status; first-year inspection exemption; 15% plug-and-play space reserved for women entrepreneurs
policies.status: In force to 2030; JaKeGA is registering authority
policies.application_status: Via single window / IT Department

policies.name: J&K Film Policy 2021
policies.what_companies_get: Fiscal incentive up to Rs 2 crore per film/TV show/web series/documentary; no location charges; single-window shooting clearances; film-city development
policies.status: Effective 05.08.2021 till 2026
policies.application_status: Film Shooting Subsidy Calculator live on portal

policies.name: Sector policies: Poultry Policy 2020; Wool Processing, Handloom & Handicrafts Policy 2020; Housing/Township Policy 2020
policies.what_companies_get: Poultry (10 yrs from 11.03.2020): 30% capital investment incentive max Rs 50 lakh; 100% DG-set subsidy max Rs 4.5 lakh; 3% working-capital interest incentive 5 yrs on expansion; 100% P&M insurance-premium reimbursement 5 yrs; 50% incinerator subsidy max Rs 1 lakh. Wool/Handloom/Handicraft: 10% export subsidy; one-time working-capital subsidy 10% (max Rs 5 lakh); 50% e-platform marketing support; artisan credit enhancements. Housing/Township: compensatory FAR 100% for EWS build-up, land-use conversion and fee exemptions for developers
policies.status: All 10-year policies from 2020, in force
policies.application_status: Via respective departments through single window

site_access_issues: investinassam.com is NOT a government site — the domain has been squatted/expired and now hosts an unrelated internet-safety blog with online-gambling links. The real Assam investment single-window is eodb.assam.gov.in.
jkindustriescommerce.nic.in: unreachable (no HTTP/HTTPS response) — all J&K verification done via singlewindow.jk.gov.in instead.

dcimanipur.gov.in (correct Manipur dept domain per search): DNS resolves to NIC 164.100.234.15 but connection refused on 443 and silent on 80 at verification time; Manipur entries therefore unverified. dtci.manipur.gov.in and commerceandindustries.mn.gov.in are NXDOMAIN.
dic.nagaland.gov.in and industries.nagaland.gov.in are NXDOMAIN; the live official domain is industry.nagaland.gov.in.

industries.arunachal.gov.in serves a React/Vite JS shell with no server-rendered content; not WordPress so the /cms/wp-json headless probe is inapplicable — no policy content extractable without a rendering browser.
industriescom.assam.gov.in/portlets/industrial-policies returns 404 (page moved); policy documents live under /documents-detail/ paths and on eodb.assam.gov.in instead.
Tripura industries.tripura.gov.in/state-incentives renders as a navigation shell to curl; detailed state incentive schedule sits in JS-loaded subpages/PDFs.

J&K portal internal inconsistency: NCSS Working Capital Interest Subvention stated as 5% / max Rs 1 cr on the highlights slide but 5.5% / max Rs 5 cr in 5 years on the scheme table of the same official PDF.
schemes_found_on_site_not_in_brief: Assam: Agarwood Promotion Policy 2020, Handicraft Policy 2022, Logistics & Warehousing Policy 2022, Biotechnology Policy 2022, Tourism Policy 2022, Renewable Energy Policy 2022, Electric Vehicle Policy 2021 (+2021 operational guidelines), Poultry Sector Commercialization Policy 2024, Agroforestry Policy 2024, State Township Policy 2023, Vehicle Scrappage Policy 2022, Sugar Policy, IT Policy, Export & Logistics Policy 2019, MSME Facilitation Act 2020, 'Scheme for incentives to industrial units for local employment generation', Credit Linked Fiscal Incentive, Freight Subsidy Scheme, Margin Money Grant
Assam Amendment 2023 mega-investment customized package (min Rs 100 cr) — bespoke negotiated incentives beyond the 2019 policy schedule
Mizoram: 'Bana Kaih' state handholding scheme and SEDP; Mizoram Procurement Preference Policy for MSEs 2025; State Logistics Policy 2022
Meghalaya: MSE Procurement Preference Policy 2020; Meghalaya Startup Policy 2018

J&K: IT/ITeS Policy 2020 (recruitment assistance Rs 26k/employee/yr up to 500), Film Policy 2021 (up to Rs 2 cr per production, live subsidy calculator), Poultry Policy 2020, Wool Processing/Handloom/Handicrafts Policy 2020, Housing & Township Policy 2020, and a Private Land Bank / land-requisition mechanism on the single window
Nagaland: 18 state-published Model DPRs to help applicants into UNNATI (incl. medical devices, cold chain, EV charging, film/VFX)
